sanskrit:
अनादिमध्यान्तमनन्तवीर्यमनन्तबाहुं शशिसूर्यनेत्रम्।
पश्यामि त्वां दीप्तहुताशवक्त्रं स्वतेजसा विश्वमिदं तपन्तम्॥
transliteration: anādi-madhyāntam ananta-vīryam ananta-bāhuṁ śaśi-sūrya-netram / paśyāmi tvāṁ dīpta-hutāśa-vaktraṁ sva-tejasā viśvam idaṁ tapantam
literal_meaning: I see You—without beginning, middle, or end; of infinite valor; with unlimited arms; having the moon and sun for eyes; with blazing fire issuing from Your mouth; scorching this universe with Your own radiance.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna confronts the archetype of the Self: a boundless, trans-personal reality that dwarfs egoic identity. The vision collapses linear time (no past-present-future) and ordinary space (infinite arms/eyes), evoking awe, dread, and the dissolution of defensive boundaries. It is the psyche’s first full encounter with the numinous, where personal narrative is relativized by an impersonal, creative-destructive force.
psychological_state: Peak trans-rational experience: simultaneous attraction and dread toward the archetypal Wholeness that transcends egoic control.
trigger_scenario: Standing before an MRI that reveals the vastness of a brain tumor, or watching wildfire consume your hometown—moments when personal story is swallowed by an impersonal, radiant force.
anchor_text: Meet the boundless Self that burns away every story you call 'me.'
